danger_signals.visual_form: Transition to 3D isometric battle view; enemy appearance on screen
danger_signals.meaning: Player has entered combat; danger is imminent

danger_signals.visual_form: Enemies visible in dungeon pathways during top-down exploration
danger_signals.meaning: Hazard zone; random battles likely to trigger

danger_signals.visual_form: MP depletion; character position relative to enemies

safety_signals.visual_form: Populated settlements with NPCs and shops; transition from dungeon to town
safety_signals.meaning: Safe zone; no random battles; access to vendors and healing

safety_signals.visual_form: Characters present in party roster (up to 8 recruitable characters visible in UI)
safety_signals.meaning: Increased combat capability; safety through numbers

safety_signals.visual_form: Consumable items and equipment displayed in town vendors
safety_signals.meaning: Opportunity to resupply; preparation possible before danger zones

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Federation forces encountered as hostile entities in conflict zones
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Locations associated with space-faring technology and advanced civilization

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Distinct enemy design differentiating them from Federation forces
Non-combatant NPCs in towns (Kratus and others)
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Towns and settled areas; medieval/fantasy aesthetic in contrast to sci-fi elements

world_state_to_visual.world_state: War between galactic powers in progress
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Presence of advanced technology mixed with medieval civilization; enemy encounters representing conflicting factions
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: NPCs in towns react to the conflict; characters seek cure for disease caused by war effects

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Plot-critical story elements communicated through dialogue and NPC interactions; motivation for exploration
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Party seeks cure through progression; quest structure drives exploration of dangerous areas

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Narrative exposition through cutscenes and story progression; cause of war revealed through plot development
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Story branches based on 'Private Actions' and affection system; character recruitment depends on previous actions

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Private Actions system allows characters to split in towns and pursue individual storylines
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Optional character interactions; different party compositions available based on relationship choices

environmental_storytelling.example: Kratus and other towns present as inhabited settlements; contrast between safe town areas and dangerous dungeons/pathways
Medieval fantasy aesthetic of Roak world mixed with sci-fi elements from galactic federation; visual clash communicates collision of worlds
Player must navigate dungeons and interact with NPCs to uncover war cause; world-state revealed through environmental exploration
Only 8 of 10 available characters recruitible per playthrough; party composition reflects story decisions, influencing world-state perception

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Can move freely, split from party for Private Actions, interact with NPCs and vendors
Random battle encounters trigger; combat transitions to 3D isometric view
Join party automatically or conditionally based on party size and story decisions
Execute preset strategies automatically; move toward closest enemy; perform attacks without direct player control
Appear in dungeons/pathways; engage in real-time combat; represent faction opposition

notes: Star Ocean uses a hybrid communication system: narrative exposition (dialogue, story progression) combined with environmental design (town vs. dungeon contrast) and mechanical signals (random battles, character recruitment). The affection system ('Private Actions') allows optional story branches that manifest as character splits in towns, indicating character agency and relationship depth. World-state regarding the galactic war is communicated primarily through plot and NPC dialogue rather than visual environmental markers. The game's sci-fi/fantasy aesthetic clash visually communicates the premise of advanced civilization encountering a medieval world.
